title: AstraZeneca CEO defends 'fantastic' Alexion buyout in face of $753M hit from dropped drug
summary: AstraZeneca is taking a $753 million impairment charge after abandoning vemircopan, an anti-C5 complement inhibitor from its $39 billion Alexion acquisition, due to lack of efficacy in lupus nephritis and impaired hepatic function indications. CEO Pascal Soriot defended the Alexion acquisition as 'fantastic' despite the broader pipeline reset that includes terminating additional programs from the Alexion and Fusion Pharmaceuticals portfolios.
